content.text: Cost of Attendance: The Full Picture
Tuition is usually the first number families see, but it's only one piece of the puzzle. The full Cost of Attendance (COA) includes:
That's everything it takes to get through one year.
Depending on the school, non-tuition costs — housing, meals, and everyday living expenses — can account for
. This is especially true at in-state public universities where tuition is relatively low.
Living costs are fairly similar no matter where your student attends. Whether it's a state school or a private university, expect to pay roughly
for housing and meals. The real pricing differences between schools come down to tuition.

attrs.html:
Visual 1 · COA Breakdown
Living costs stay constant — tuition drives the difference
attrs.deps:
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.5.0/css/all.min.css">
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Aleo:wght@400;500;600&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
:root {
  --color-primary-teal: #4F788D;
  --color-primary-green: #6B9D81;
  --color-primary-sea-glass: #B0C8C0;
  --color-accent-saffron: #E6B830;
  --color-accent-coral-red: #D4604A;
  --color-dark-forest: #2E5E4A;
  --color-dark-navy-ink: #1E3A5F;
  --color-bg: #F7F9F8;
  --color-text: #586068;
  --color-text-emphasis: #2C3E4A;
  --font-heading: 'Aleo', serif;
  --font-body: 'Inter', sans-serif;
}

* { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

body {
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  font-size: 16px;
  line-height: 1.6;
  color: var(--color-text);
  background: var(--color-bg);
  padding: 32px 16px;
}

.container {
  max-width: 880px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  background: #FFFFFF;
  padding: 56px 64px;
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.04);
}

h1 {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 2.25rem;
  line-height: 1.25;
  color: var(--color-primary-teal);
  margin-bottom: 24px;
}

h2 {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 1.75rem;
  line-height: 1.25;
  color: var(--color-dark-forest);
  margin-top: 48px;
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

h3 {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 400;
  font-size: 1.375rem;
  line-height: 1.3;
  color: var(--color-primary-teal);
  margin-top: 32px;
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

p {
  margin-bottom: 24px;
}

ul {
  margin-bottom: 24px;
  padding-left: 24px;
}

ul li {
  margin-bottom: 12px;
  list-style: none;
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 24px;
}

ul li::before {
  content: "\f00c";
  font-family: "Font Awesome 6 Free";
  font-weight: 400;
  color: var(--color-primary-green);
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  top: 2px;
}

strong, .emphasis {
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--color-text-emphasis);
}

/* Chart containers */
.chart-card {
  background: var(--color-bg);
  border-left: 4px solid var(--color-primary-green);
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 24px;
  margin: 32px 0;
}

.chart-card .chart-label {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: var(--color-dark-forest);
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.chart-card .chart-title {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 1.25rem;
  color: var(--color-primary-teal);
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.chart-wrapper {
  position: relative;
  height: 340px;
  margin-top: 16px;
}

.chart-wrapper.tall { height: 360px; }
.chart-wrapper.short { height: 280px; }

/* Table */
.table-wrapper {
  overflow-x: auto;
  margin: 24px 0 32px;
  border-radius: 6px;
  border: 1px solid rgba(176, 200, 192, 0.5);
}

table {
  width: 100%;
  border-collapse: collapse;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}

thead th {
  background: var(--color-primary-teal);
  color: #FFFFFF;
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  font-weight: 600;
  text-align: left;
  padding: 14px 16px;
}

tbody td {
  padding: 14px 16px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(176, 200, 192, 0.4);
  color: var(--color-text);
}

tbody tr:nth-child(even) td {
  background: rgba(176, 200, 192, 0.1);
}

tbody tr:last-child td {
  border-bottom: none;
}

tbody td:first-child {
  font-weight: 500;
  color: var(--color-text-emphasis);
}

/* Net Price Formula */
.formula {
  background: rgba(176, 200, 192, 0.18);
  border: 1px solid var(--color-primary-sea-glass);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 32px 24px;
  text-align: center;
  margin: 24px 0 32px;
}

.formula-equation {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 1.25rem;
  color: var(--color-dark-forest);
  line-height: 1.6;
}

.formula-equation .op {
  color: var(--color-accent-coral-red);
  font-size: 1.5rem;
  margin: 0 8px;
  vertical-align: middle;
}

.formula-equation .term {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 6px 14px;
  background: #FFFFFF;
  border-radius: 4px;
  margin: 4px;
  border: 1px solid var(--color-primary-sea-glass);
}

.formula-equation .net {
  background: var(--color-primary-green);
  color: #FFFFFF;
  border-color: var(--color-primary-green);
}

/* Comparison */
.comparison {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 20px;
  margin: 24px 0 32px;
}

.comparison-card {
  background: #FFFFFF;
  border-top: 4px solid var(--color-primary-teal);
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 24px;
  box-shadow: 0 1px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
}

.comparison-card.public {
  border-top-color: var(--color-accent-saffron);
}

.comparison-card h4 {
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 1.125rem;
  color: var(--color-text-emphasis);
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.comparison-step {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 10px 0;
  border-bottom: 1px dashed rgba(176, 200, 192, 0.6);
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}

.comparison-step:last-of-type { border-bottom: none; }

.comparison-step .label { color: var(--color-text); }
.comparison-step .value { font-weight: 600; color: var(--color-text-emphasis); }

.comparison-net {
  margin-top: 16px;
  background: var(--color-primary-green);
  color: #FFFFFF;
  padding: 14px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  text-align: center;
  font-family: var(--font-heading);
  font-weight: 500;
  font-size: 1.125rem;
}

.comparison-card.public .comparison-net {
  background: var(--color-dark-forest);
}

/* Callout link */
.cta-link {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 12px;
  background: rgba(230, 184, 48, 0.15);
  border-left: 4px solid var(--color-accent-saffron);
  padding: 16px 20px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  margin: 24px 0 32px;
  text-decoration: none;
  color: var(--color-text-emphasis);
  font-weight: 500;
  transition: background 0.2s ease;
}

.cta-link:hover {
  background: rgba(230, 184, 48, 0.25);
}

.cta-link i {
  color: var(--color-dark-forest);
  font-size: 1.1rem;
}

/* Intro lede */
.lede {
  font-size: 1.125rem;
  color: var(--color-text-emphasis);
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(176, 200, 192, 0.5);
  padding-bottom: 32px;
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

@media (max-width: 700px) {
  .container { padding: 32px 24px; }
  h1 { font-size: 1.75rem; }
  h2 { font-size: 1.375rem; }
  .comparison { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .formula-equation { font-size: 1rem; }
}
</style>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js"></script>

attrs.html:
Visual 2 · Where Does the Money Go?
A typical in-state public — tuition is only part of it
See what your family might actually owe after aid: use a college's Net Price Calculator to estimate the real cost of attending.

content.text: College prices vary widely depending on the type of school. Here's a general sense of where things stand, so your family has a frame of reference as you explore options.
. Schools like USC and NYU are at or approaching $100,000. That sounds steep — but private schools often have the most financial aid available, which can bring the actual cost down significantly.
. If your student attends a state flagship in your home state, this is usually the lowest sticker price available.
. Without the in-state tuition benefit, families pay a premium — and most out-of-state publics offer little to no need-based aid to non-residents.

attrs.html:
School Type Sticker Price Typical Aid Net Price 4-Year Cost
Private University $85K–$100K $40K–$55K $45K–$55K $180K–$220K
In-State Public $25K–$40K $0–$10K $20K–$35K $80K–$140K
Out-of-State Public $50K–$80K $0–$5K $45K–$75K $180K–$300K

Visual 3 · Price Range Spectrum
Where each school type falls on the cost continuum

content.text: Non-tuition costs (housing, meals, books, travel) run roughly
. When comparing colleges, make sure you're looking at the full Cost of Attendance — not just tuition.

attrs.html: See how generous a college is with need-based aid by searching the Need-Based Financial Aid Data.

content.text: This is one of the most important things to understand early:
most students do not pay the full sticker price.
The majority receive some combination of need-based grants, merit scholarships, or both.
is what your family actually pays after that free money — grants and scholarships — is subtracted. This is the number that matters when comparing schools.

attrs.html: Sticker Price (Full COA) − Grants & Scholarships = Net Price

content.text: Here's how this plays out in practice. A private university with a $99,000 sticker price might award $50,000 in grants and scholarships. Your family's net price:
. An in-state public at $35,000 with no aid costs exactly
. In this scenario, the "expensive" school actually costs more — but for some families, the private school could be the better deal.

attrs.html:
Private University

Sticker Price$99,000
Grants & Aid−$50,000
Net Price: $49,000

In-State Public

Sticker Price$35,000
Grants & Aid−$0
Net Price: $35,000

content.text: never eliminate a school based on sticker price alone. Research each school's aid policies and run their net price calculator to see what your family would actually pay.

attrs.html: Find out where your family stands: use the Student Aid Index (SAI) Calculator to estimate your expected family contribution.
